body.thing: the router decomposition invariant: adding a model<->provider connection requires ZERO rule edits and dispatch resolves to it
body.intention: I intend that registering one Connection for an existing model, on a provider no rule ever named, makes that model dispatchable through the real dispatch path (domain.select) with no rule written and no ModelSpec touched — so the four stacks cannot silently re-collapse into a monolith

red_run.summary:
engine._resolve_connections().register(
            Connection(EXISTING_MODEL, NEW_PROVIDER, 0.0)
        )
    
        decision = coding.select(engine, task_class="worker")
    
>       assert decision is not None, (
            "adding a connection for an existing model must make it dispatchable with ZERO rule "
            "edits — a None here means dispatch is still reading a rule stack that names models"
        )
E       AssertionError: adding a connection for an existing model must make it dispatchable with ZERO rule edits — a None here means dispatch is still reading a rule stack that names models

body.why: In the monolith, reachability was smeared across ModelSpec.source_name AND the (task_class, model_id, source_name) rule triples, so making an existing model reachable on a new provider meant EDITING RULES — a registered Connection was inert because dispatch never read the connections stack. This invariant is the runnable guard the original rot would have failed.
